title: Mutual Suspicion
description: Two rivals have each published a wrong theory. You know the truth about both ingredients — find a mix that puts both publications under suspicion at the same time.

hints.text: The question asks for a conflict without removal: a master mix where both publications are contradicted simultaneously, but the audience can't tell which rival is lying — so neither publication gets removed. Look at both wrong publications and find a pair whose true mix conflicts with both claimed alchemicals.

Ingredient 1 is truly npN, but publication 1 claims pNn. Ingredient 2 is truly pnP, but publication 2 claims Nnp. Mix the two: the true result is neutral. Under pub1: mix(pNn, pnP) = G− ≠ neutral → conflict on pub1. Under pub2: mix(npN, Nnp) = R− ≠ neutral → conflict on pub2. Both publications conflict — the audience can't determine which is wrong.

One master debunk: mix ingredient 1 + ingredient 2. True result: neutral. Expected if pub1 correct: G−. Expected if pub2 correct: R−. Both differ from neutral → both conflict → neither publication is removed. That is the conflict-only outcome the puzzle asks for.
